summary: Diff adds a Motoko backend with profiles/listings/orders/messages types and many frontend pages/hooks for II login, onboarding, marketplace browsing, listing CRUD UI, order placement, sales/purchases, and an order message thread. However, several BuildRequest acceptance criteria are either contradicted by shown backend access rules or not evidenced in the diff (notably public farmer profiles, order cancellation + Cancelled status, structured backend errors, and upgrade persistence).

missing_requirements.requirement: Public profile view is available for farmers from listings (shows display name, location, bio).
missing_requirements.reason: Backend `getUserProfile` explicitly forbids viewing other users' profiles unless admin; frontend attempts to fetch farmer profile from listing, which would trap under this access rule.
evidence: backend/main.mo (getUserProfile: traps unless caller==user or admin)

missing_requirements.requirement: Buyer can cancel a Pending request; Purchases statuses include Pending, Accepted, Declined, Cancelled; status changes persisted.
missing_requirements.reason: Backend `OrderRequest.status` variant shown only includes `#pending`, `#accepted`, `#declined` (no `#cancelled`). No cancel operation is evidenced in frontend hooks/pages shown.

missing_requirements.requirement: Backend rejects invalid inputs and returns structured errors that the frontend displays.
missing_requirements.reason: Backend validation shown uses `Runtime.trap("...")` with plain text strings rather than a structured error type/variant; no structured error handling is evidenced.
evidence: backend/main.mo (createListing: Runtime.trap("Missing or invalid required fields"))

missing_requirements.requirement: Data remains available after canister upgrades/restarts within the template’s standard persistence approach.
missing_requirements.reason: Diff shows in-memory `var` counters and `Map.empty(...)` collections, but no evidence of stable storage (`stable var`) and/or `preupgrade`/`postupgrade` logic in the visible portion. With truncated backend, persistence may exist later, but it is not evidenced in the provided diff summary.
evidence: backend/main.mo (var nextListingId/nextOrderId; Map.empty for storage; no persistence logic shown in visible content)

hallucinated_features.description: Admin-secret based access-control initialization via URL parameter `caffeineAdminToken` and backend `_initializeAccessControlWithSecret` call.
hallucinated_features.reason: BuildRequest does not mention admin roles/tokens or secret URL parameters; this is additional auth/authorization behavior beyond Internet Identity session awareness.
evidence: backend/main.mo (imports AccessControl + MixinAuthorization; includes authorization mixin)
